# Device-to-Host Notification Vectors

These vectors characterize SDK-level device-to-host notification mapping before shared ownership extraction. Each vector stores the notification id and serialized parameter bytes that should be emitted unchanged while parser-specific fields are decoded into typed protobuf payloads when supported. `D2hNotificationCommonPolicyTest.kt` now executes shared notification ID mapping, unknown-ID filtering, raw parameter preservation, repeated-notification ordering, invalid-payload null parsing, selected typed field decoding, and `mapping-readiness.json` behavior-family coverage before production D2H notification mapping moves. `D2hStreamRuntimePolicyCommonTest.kt` now executes shared fake-stream policy for emitted values before late upstream errors, consumer cancellation cleanup, failed-subscribe observer behavior, and suppression of later notifications after cancellation; stream orchestration can move only after this policy is compile-verified and platform facade error mapping remains pinned.

`mapping-readiness.json` names the shared-contract behavior-family gate for D2H notification mapping: known notification ID mapping, unknown notification ID filtering, raw parameter preservation, typed field decoding for filesystem, sync-required, autosync, and start-GPS notifications, stop-GPS empty parameters, invalid-payload null parsing, repeated-notification ordering, platform mapping vector references, and the `compile-verification-gate` must remain covered before production D2H notification mapping moves into shared code.

`stream-runtime-readiness.json` names the shared-contract behavior-family gate for D2H stream orchestration and links back to `stream-runtime-policy.json`: mapped values emitted before late upstream errors, late-error propagation, consumer cancellation, suppression after cancellation, unknown-notification filtering, continuation after unknown notifications, failed subscribe with no observer, observer cleanup, public facade error mapping, platform facade vector references, and the `compile-verification-gate` must remain covered before production D2H stream runtime code moves into shared.
